mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the median forward return post-trigger is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and acceleration in consumer prices indicates a supply-shock inflation environment that erodes real corporate margins and forces equity de-rating, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO falls and CPIAUCSL rises concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of peak consumer credit expansion, a subsequent deceleration in retail sales growth acts as a leading indicator of aggregate demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when RSAFS growth decelerates following a credit peak, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals aggressive monetary tightening that immediately raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are non-negative in more than half of the triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, maintaining positive equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or if the rolling Spearman correlation falls below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of housing starts and rising unemployment reflects a synchronized cooling in both consumer durables demand and labor market tightness, which systematically reduces forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both HOUST declines and UNRATE rises, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for GDP growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macro regime, a sudden spike in industrial production indices reflects pent-up demand release rather than sustainable capacity expansion, triggering short-term profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following INDPRO spikes during supply-constrained periods, or if the signal shows no predictive edge over a random baseline.
